title: Hanging Wood Wall Bins
summary: A wall-hung shelf unit with notched sides and five open front bins, cut efficiently from a single sheet of 1/4" plywood.
description:
The notches that hold each shelf tier are cut directly into the two solid 1x6 sides — trace the shelf board's actual width onto the side piece first, rather than working from a fixed measurement, so the notch matches your real stock instead of a nominal size that may not match what you bought. Sand the cut edges of every notch smooth before assembly; a rough jigsaw edge is hard to reach once the shelf is glued into place.

All five of the plywood front pieces and the back panel come off a single 24" x 48" sheet if you lay out the cuts as shown — a genuinely efficient use of material for a shelf this size, with almost nothing left over.

tools.note: Alternative to 2" construction screws for the shelf joints.
Cuts the shelf notches into the side pieces.

cutList.note: Notched to fit the shelf bottoms — trace your actual 1x3 stock width for the notch, not a nominal size.

steps.title: Cut the plywood pieces
steps.body: Lay out and cut the back panel and the five front pieces from a single 24" x 48" sheet of 1/4" plywood, as shown in your reference layout. The back comes out slightly smaller than the full opening, but since the 1x2 hanging cleat covers that area, it won't matter. Use a circular saw or table saw for these cuts.

steps.title: Notch the sides
steps.body: Place a shelf-bottom board on top of a side board with one edge flush, and trace a line down the side board to mark the notch width. Mark the notch depth, then carefully cut the notches out with a jigsaw. Sand the cut edges smooth.

steps.title: Attach the shelf bottoms
steps.body: Attach the two sides together with the shelf-bottom boards, seated in the notches. Use either 3/4" pocket holes with 1-1/4" pocket-hole screws, or 2" construction screws.

steps.title: Attach the hanging cleat
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 cleat to the top the same way you attached the shelves. You'll use this cleat to hang the finished unit on the wall.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back panel to the assembled unit with glue and finish nails.

steps.title: Attach the front pieces
steps.body: Glue and nail the front pieces on, using 3/4" brad nails.

images.alt: Wall-hung shelf unit with notched sides and five open bins
